name: Top-Down Camera
description: Top-Down Camera is a viewpoint behavior that frames action and communicates spatial information to the player.
design_notes: Top-Down Camera should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For camera mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use top-down camera as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect top-down camera to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels oriented and confident because the game frames what matters.

parameters.name: follow_lag
parameters.description: How far behind the target the camera is allowed to feel.

parameters.name: framing_offset
parameters.description: Offset used to keep the important subject away from dead center.

parameters.name: transition_speed
parameters.description: How quickly the camera blends to the new behavior.

edge_cases: Top-Down Camera is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Top-Down Camera overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Top-Down Camera runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Top-Down Camera state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Top-Down Camera plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Top-Down Camera produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Top-Down Camera strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Top-Down Camera becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Top-Down Camera with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Top-Down Camera as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Top-Down Camera with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Top-Down Camera as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
